title: pilosella schultesii subsp. mendelii
intro: Pilosella schultesii subsp. mendelii, also known as Mendel's ciliate hawkweed, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential candidate for medicinal applications. The health benefits of Pilosella schultesii subsp. mendelii include its potential to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, and it may also have antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by extracting the sesquiterpene lactones using solvents such as ethanol or glycerin.
